description: Back on the Zephyr, Mack and Sousa face off with Chronicom Hunters attempting to take their faces. Mack uses an electrified device to subdue them. Sousa recalls their previous encounter at Area 51 and proposes they rig the defeated Chronicoms into a bomb, but Mack counters with a safer missile plan for escape.
location: Zephyr One (S.H.I.E.L.D. quinjet / mobile command plane)

transcript.line: When I asked if you had something else, I meant something... bigger and more powerful.
Well, I don't see you coming up with any brilliant ideas.
Wish I could disagree.
Hey, look, for a guy who was ripped from his own timeline, who ended up on a spaceship full of robots, you're not doing half bad. Follow my lead.
We are here to take your faces.
Oh. Lord knows you could do a lot better than the ones you're wearing now.
I'm pretty sure they don't understand sarcasm.
They'll understand this.
If that wasn't egg in your beer. How many more of those do you have?
That's it. We just have to hold them off long enough for Daisy to get back with Simmons and Deke.
And then? One problem at a time.
That's not creepy...
He should make it.
Don't sound so thrilled.
Yo-Yo.
What happened?
It was dark. Speared myself with a piece of rebar. And there's a lot of rebar. As far as I can see, we're trapped down here. All the exits are blocked under a ton of rubble.
Son of a bitch.
Have a little respect for the people who saved your ass.
I meant Nathaniel Malick. Bastard left me here to die. What ever happened to team loyalty? I was shown a future where S.H.I.E.L.D. abandoned me. How come it's playing out the exact opposite?
Malick showed you an old timeline. This one's different. Nothing's written. It's not too late to be on the right side of history.
Looks like I'm wearing a white hat from now on. Hey. Buddy?
Interesting. I'm hearing a pattern in the sounds.
